name: Wobbleplum
concept: A plump translucent raspberry-jelly creature crusted in sugar who hugs its own belly with both stubby hands, because a little heart-shaped pit of darker jelly is suspended inside it and it doesn't want it to slosh.
imagePrompt:
Die-cut sticker illustration of an original chubby jelly-candy creature, full body, standing, facing viewer, symmetrical and stable.

SILHOUETTE: one single connected blob-body — a fat gumdrop dome, wide and heavy at the base, narrowing to a soft rounded peak that flops gently to one side like a scoop of set jelly, giving a frozen mid-wobble tilt. The base spreads into two short stumpy jelly feet fused to the body (no gaps). Two short rounded stub-arms curve forward and rest flat on its own belly, hands touching each other so the arms read as one thick unbroken outline. Silhouette is chunky, closed, instantly readable at thumbnail size — no thin strands, no spikes, no detached parts, nothing narrower than a finger-thickness.

MATERIAL: translucent gel. The body is glassy jelly you can see into: deep raspberry-magenta at the edges, glowing warm amber-coral where light passes through the thinner top, with a soft internal gradient like backlit fruit gum. Thick glossy highlights — one big soft crescent gleam on the upper left of the dome, a small round dot highlight — painted as clean solid shapes, not airbrush. A slightly darker jelly rim marks the bottom where the gel is densest.

SUGAR CRUST: a chunky granular crust of pale pink-white sugar coats the top of the dome, the shoulders and the tops of the feet, like frost on a hill. Render the sugar as bold, deliberate crystal chunks — visible cubes and pebbles of sugar, each big and blocky, forming a slightly bumpy but still clean crust edge. Never fine sparkle, never dust.

THE ONE IDEA: suspended deep inside the translucent belly, clearly visible through the jelly, is a smooth rounded heart-shaped pit of darker jam-red — its little core. The creature's two hands press protectively against the belly right in front of it, cradling it. This is the whole character: a candy carefully holding its own heart still.

FACE: set in the upper front of the dome, reading as inclusions inside the gel. Two very large, glossy, honest black oval eyes, wide-set, each with one big white catchlight and a tiny second one. No eyebrows. A small, simple, soft mouth — a tiny rounded open smile in a deeper berry tone, slightly off-centre so it looks bashful. Two soft blush ovals of pale sugar-pink low on the cheeks. Expression: gentle, earnest, a bit anxious about the wobbling — the face of something you want to look after.

PROPORTIONS: head-and-body are one shape, roughly 3 heads tall total but really just one big body; face occupies the top third; arms and feet are tiny relative to the mass, exaggerating softness and weight.

PALETTE: one bold colour dominates — raspberry-magenta jelly — supported only by warm amber inner glow, jam-red core, pale sugar white-pink crust, black eyes, white highlights. No other hues.

STYLE: modern die-cut vinyl sticker. Bold uniform thick white sticker border hugging the outer silhouette, a subtle second darker outline just inside it, flat confident cel shading with two tones plus highlight, crisp vector-clean edges, soft printed drop shadow under the feet fused to the body. No text, no symbols, no floating particles, no sparkles, no motion lines. Cheerful, huggable, wholesome, completely original character design.
